title: Metrics Tab with Spreadsheet Component and Metadata Fields
story: As a data analyst, I want to create, edit, and view a list of metrics in a dedicated Metrics tab using a spreadsheet-style interface, so that I can manage my organization's metric definitions alongside existing Migration, Dictionary, and Entity Relationship tools.
description:
## Overview

Introduces a new Metrics tab/page in DataTools Pro alongside the existing Migration, Dictionary, and Entity Relationships views. Users can quickly create, edit, and delete metrics using an inline spreadsheet component.

## Data Table Display

The Metrics data table displays the following columns:

- **Metric Name**
- **Definition** — displayed as comma- or semicolon-separated text (read-only display)
- **Line of Business** — displayed as text, values sourced from taxonomy assignments
- **Topic** — displayed as text, values sourced from taxonomy assignments

## Database Fields

The underlying Metrics table stores the following attributes:

- Metric Name
- Definition
- Logic
- Date Created
- Last Modified Date
- Business Owner (varchar)
- Related Date or Time (foreign key linked to a Salesforce field)
- Target Interval (varchar)

## Taxonomy Assignment

Line of Business and Topic values are assigned by mapping taxonomy values to individual metrics, similar to the pattern used in the Migration module. A metric can have one or multiple taxonomy values assigned.

## Deletion

Metric deletion uses soft delete to preserve historical data.
acceptance_criteria: A new Metrics tab appears in the main navigation alongside Migration, Dictionary, and Entity Relationships
User can view a data table listing all metrics with columns: Metric Name, Definition, Line of Business, and Topic
User can create a new metric inline using the spreadsheet component
User can edit existing metric fields inline
User can delete a metric (soft delete preserves the record)
Line of Business and Topic columns display taxonomy values assigned to each metric
Multiple taxonomy values can be assigned to a single metric for Line of Business and Topic
All database fields are persisted: Metric Name, Definition, Logic, Date Created, Last Modified Date, Business Owner, Related Date or Time, Target Interval
ux_changes: Adds a new Metrics tab to the main application navigation. Displays metrics in a spreadsheet-style data table with inline CRUD operations. Definition, Line of Business, and Topic columns render comma/semicolon-separated text values.
middleware_changes: Server-side CRUD endpoints for metrics: create, read, update, and soft delete. Taxonomy assignment logic maps Line of Business and Topic values to individual metrics.
data_model_changes: New Metrics table with columns: Metric Name, Definition, Logic, Date Created, Last Modified Date, Business Owner (varchar), Related Date or Time (FK to Salesforce field), Target Interval (varchar). Soft delete flag for record preservation. Taxonomy mapping table links metrics to Line of Business and Topic category values.
api_changes: CRUD API endpoints for the Metrics resource (create, read, update, soft delete). Taxonomy assignment endpoint to map Line of Business and Topic values to metrics.
implementation_notes: Metrics page hosted at /apps/metrics. CRUD operations implemented with AJAX. Delete uses soft delete to preserve records. Taxonomy assignment to metrics follows the same pattern as migration taxonomy mapping. A separate story will cover the Metric popout/detail window.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed the metrics page with add/update/delete CRUD and soft delete implementation. Discussion clarified that Line of Business and Topic values come from taxonomy mapping (not free text), following the same pattern used in Migration. Developer asked whether taxonomy assignment should be a separate task; product owner confirmed it should be covered within this ticket.
